model_narrative: We run a food manufacturing operation that refines and blends raw oils into a final product over a sequence of months. The raw oils come in two kinds, vegetable and non-vegetable. Each month we decide how much of each raw oil to buy, how much to refine into the blend, how much to keep in storage, and which oils to include in that month's blend. Buying raw oils costs money at prices that vary by oil and by month, holding oil in storage incurs a per-ton monthly charge, and the finished product sells at a fixed price per ton. The objective is to maximize the total net profit over the whole horizon.

sets.name: m
sets.doc: the planning periods given as months in chronological order; the first member is the opening month and each later member follows the one before it, and the sequence is treated as a cycle so the month before the first month is the last month

sets.name: p
sets.doc: the raw oils available for refining and blending

sets.name: pv
sets.doc: the subset of raw oils that are vegetable oils

sets.name: pnv
sets.doc: the subset of raw oils that are non-vegetable oils; the member o3 is the non-vegetable oil that vegetable oils depend on

params.name: maxstore
params.doc: the maximum amount of any single raw oil that can be held in storage at the end of a month, in tons

params.name: maxusepv
params.doc: the maximum total amount of vegetable oils that can be refined in a single month, in tons

params.name: maxusepnv
params.doc: the maximum total amount of non-vegetable oils that can be refined in a single month, in tons

params.name: minusep
params.doc: the minimum amount of a raw oil that must be used in a month if that oil is used at all in that month, in tons

params.name: maxnusep
params.doc: the maximum number of distinct raw oils that may be used together in a single month's blend

params.name: sp
params.doc: the sales price of the final refined and blended oil, in dollars per ton

params.name: sc
params.doc: the storage cost charged for each ton of raw oil held in storage for one month, in dollars per ton per month

params.name: hmin
params.doc: the minimum allowed hardness of the final refined product, in hardness units

params.name: hmax
params.doc: the maximum allowed hardness of the final refined product, in hardness units

params.name: stock
params.doc: the required storage level of each raw oil at the end of the final month, in tons; this also represents the starting stock held before the horizon

params.name: h
params.doc: the hardness of each raw oil, in hardness units; hardness blends linearly by the amount of each oil used

params.name: cost
params.doc: the purchase cost of each raw oil in each month, in dollars per ton

vars.name: produce
vars.doc: the amount of final refined and blended oil produced in each month, in tons

vars.name: use
vars.doc: the amount of each raw oil refined into the blend in each month, in tons

vars.name: induse
vars.doc: indicator that equals 1 if a raw oil is used in a month's blend and 0 otherwise

vars.name: buy
vars.doc: the amount of each raw oil purchased in each month, in tons

vars.name: store
vars.doc: the amount of each raw oil held in storage at the end of each month, in tons; bounded above by the storage capacity, and fixed in the final month to the required end stock

vars.name: profit
vars.doc: the total net profit over the whole horizon, in dollars

constraints.name: profit
constraints.intent: Total profit over the whole horizon must equal the revenue from selling all the finished product, minus the cost of buying raw oils, minus the cost of storing raw oils. Value the product made each month at its sales price, subtract the cost of every raw oil bought in each month at that month's purchase price, and subtract the storage charge applied to every raw oil held in storage at the end of each month. Set the total profit equal to this net amount.

constraints.name: use[mm,
constraints.intent: In each month the amount of vegetable oils refined into the blend is limited. For each month, the total vegetable oil used cannot exceed the monthly limit on vegetable oil refining.

constraints.name: use[mm,
constraints.intent: In each month the amount of non-vegetable oils refined into the blend is limited. For each month, the total non-vegetable oil used cannot exceed the monthly limit on non-vegetable oil refining.

constraints.name: produce[mm]
constraints.intent: The amount of finished product made each month is exactly the total of all raw oils refined that month. For each month, set the production equal to the sum of every raw oil used.

constraints.name: h[pp]
constraints.intent: The finished product each month must be at least as hard as the minimum allowed hardness. For each month, the hardness contributed by the raw oils used, where each oil contributes its own hardness scaled by how much of it is used, must be at least the minimum hardness times the amount produced that month.

constraints.name: h[pp]
constraints.intent: The finished product each month must be no harder than the maximum allowed hardness. For each month, the hardness contributed by the raw oils used, where each oil contributes its own hardness scaled by how much of it is used, cannot exceed the maximum hardness times the amount produced that month.

constraints.name: m)
constraints.intent: Stored quantities of each raw oil must carry over consistently from month to month. For each raw oil and each month, the amount carried in from the end of the previous month plus the amount bought that month must equal the amount used that month plus the amount left in storage at the end of that month. The horizon wraps around, so the stock carried into the first month is the stock left at the end of the last month.

constraints.name: use[mm,
constraints.intent: If a raw oil is included in a month's blend, then at least a minimum amount of it must be used. For each raw oil and each month, the amount used must be at least the minimum usage whenever that oil is marked as used, and may be zero when it is not used.

constraints.name: maxusepv
constraints.intent: A raw oil can only be used in a month if it is marked as used that month, and when it is used its amount is capped by the relevant refining limit. For each raw oil and each month, the amount used must be zero unless the oil is marked as used, and when used it cannot exceed the monthly refining limit for that kind of oil, which is the vegetable limit for vegetable oils and the non-vegetable limit for non-vegetable oils.

constraints.name: induse[mm,
constraints.intent: The blend in any month may draw on only a limited number of distinct raw oils. For each month, the count of raw oils marked as used cannot exceed the allowed maximum number of oils in a blend.

constraints.name: induse[mm,
constraints.intent: Whenever any vegetable oil is used in a month, a particular non-vegetable oil that the vegetable oils depend on must also be used that month. For each month, the number of vegetable oils marked as used cannot exceed that dependency oil's used indicator scaled by the total number of vegetable oils, so if the dependency oil is not used then no vegetable oil may be used.

query: There's a proposal on the table to simplify the month-four batch on the floor: instead of letting it draw on three different raw oils, hold the recipe to at most two distinct oils that month. If we run the plan with the month-four blend limited to two oils, what total profit does the optimizer return?
expected_pyomo: model.tight_m4 = Constraint(expr=sum(model.induse['m4', pp] for pp in model.p) <= 2)

query: Engineering is pitching a two-part operating package and wants the combined impact. First, cap the total oil o3 fed into blends across the whole six-month plan at 450 tons to ease the strain on that one feedstock. Second, smooth the line so refined output never climbs by more than 30 tons from one month to the next. Plug both in together and walk me through what total profit the plan lands on.
expected_pyomo:
model.cap_o3_total = Constraint(expr=sum(model.use[mm, 'o3'] for mm in model.m) <= 450)
def _ramp(model, mm):
    return model.produce[mm] - model.produce[list(model.m)[list(model.m).index(mm)-1]] <= 30 if list(model.m).index(mm) > 0 else Constraint.Skip
model.ramp_prod = Constraint(model.m, rule=_ramp)

query: Procurement wants to keep our exposure to oil o2 in check given how much we lean on it. We're looking at a ceiling of 500 tons on total o2 purchased across the six months — what total profit does the model land on under that cap?
expected_pyomo: model.cap_buy_o2 = Constraint(expr=sum(model.buy[mm, 'o2'] for mm in model.m) <= 500)

query: Our cash tied up in vegetable-oil purchases keeps creeping up across the half-year. Shouldn't we just cap how much vegetable oil we buy in total over the six months at a thousand tons and stop the spend from running away? What's the model seeing that makes it lean so hard on buying vegetable feedstock?
expected_pyomo: model.cap_buy_veg = Constraint(expr=sum(model.buy[mm, pp] for mm in model.m for pp in model.pv) <= 1000)

params.name: maxnusep
params.doc: the maximum number of distinct raw oils that may be used together in a single month's blend

params.name: sp
params.doc: the sales price of the final refined and blended oil, in dollars per ton

params.name: sc
params.doc: the storage cost charged for each ton of raw oil held in storage for one month, in dollars per ton per month

params.name: hmin
params.doc: the minimum allowed hardness of the final refined product, in hardness units

params.name: hmax
params.doc: the maximum allowed hardness of the final refined product, in hardness units

params.name: stock
params.doc: the required storage level of each raw oil at the end of the final month, in tons; this also represents the starting stock held before the horizon

params.name: h
params.doc: the hardness of each raw oil, in hardness units; hardness blends linearly by the amount of each oil used

params.name: cost
params.doc: the purchase cost of each raw oil in each month, in dollars per ton

vars.name: produce
vars.doc: the amount of final refined and blended oil produced in each month, in tons

vars.name: use
vars.doc: the amount of each raw oil refined into the blend in each month, in tons

vars.name: induse
vars.doc: indicator that equals 1 if a raw oil is used in a month's blend and 0 otherwise

vars.name: buy
vars.doc: the amount of each raw oil purchased in each month, in tons

vars.name: store
vars.doc: the amount of each raw oil held in storage at the end of each month, in tons; bounded above by the storage capacity, and fixed in the final month to the required end stock

vars.name: profit
vars.doc: the total net profit over the whole horizon, in dollars

constraints.name: profit
constraints.intent: Total profit over the whole horizon must equal the revenue from selling all the finished product, minus the cost of buying raw oils, minus the cost of storing raw oils. Value the product made each month at its sales price, subtract the cost of every raw oil bought in each month at that month's purchase price, and subtract the storage charge applied to every raw oil held in storage at the end of each month. Set the total profit equal to this net amount.

constraints.name: use[mm,
constraints.intent: In each month the amount of vegetable oils refined into the blend is limited. For each month, the total vegetable oil used cannot exceed the monthly limit on vegetable oil refining.

constraints.name: use[mm,
constraints.intent: In each month the amount of non-vegetable oils refined into the blend is limited. For each month, the total non-vegetable oil used cannot exceed the monthly limit on non-vegetable oil refining.

constraints.name: produce[mm]
constraints.intent: The amount of finished product made each month is exactly the total of all raw oils refined that month. For each month, set the production equal to the sum of every raw oil used.

constraints.name: h[pp]
constraints.intent: The finished product each month must be at least as hard as the minimum allowed hardness. For each month, the hardness contributed by the raw oils used, where each oil contributes its own hardness scaled by how much of it is used, must be at least the minimum hardness times the amount produced that month.

constraints.name: h[pp]
constraints.intent: The finished product each month must be no harder than the maximum allowed hardness. For each month, the hardness contributed by the raw oils used, where each oil contributes its own hardness scaled by how much of it is used, cannot exceed the maximum hardness times the amount produced that month.

constraints.name: m)
constraints.intent: Stored quantities of each raw oil must carry over consistently from month to month. For each raw oil and each month, the amount carried in from the end of the previous month plus the amount bought that month must equal the amount used that month plus the amount left in storage at the end of that month. The horizon wraps around, so the stock carried into the first month is the stock left at the end of the last month.

constraints.name: use[mm,
constraints.intent: If a raw oil is included in a month's blend, then at least a minimum amount of it must be used. For each raw oil and each month, the amount used must be at least the minimum usage whenever that oil is marked as used, and may be zero when it is not used.

constraints.name: maxusepv
constraints.intent: A raw oil can only be used in a month if it is marked as used that month, and when it is used its amount is capped by the relevant refining limit. For each raw oil and each month, the amount used must be zero unless the oil is marked as used, and when used it cannot exceed the monthly refining limit for that kind of oil, which is the vegetable limit for vegetable oils and the non-vegetable limit for non-vegetable oils.

constraints.name: induse[mm,
constraints.intent: The blend in any month may draw on only a limited number of distinct raw oils. For each month, the count of raw oils marked as used cannot exceed the allowed maximum number of oils in a blend.

constraints.name: induse[mm,
constraints.intent: Whenever any vegetable oil is used in a month, a particular non-vegetable oil that the vegetable oils depend on must also be used that month. For each month, the number of vegetable oils marked as used cannot exceed that dependency oil's used indicator scaled by the total number of vegetable oils, so if the dependency oil is not used then no vegetable oil may be used.

query: Some months the blend tips well past half vegetable oil, which leaves us badly exposed to the veg market. Wouldn't it be obvious to hold vegetable oil to at most 40 percent of each month's refined output and balance things out? What's the tradeoff that keeps the optimizer from making that move?

model_narrative: We run a food manufacturing operation that refines and blends raw oils into a final product over a sequence of months. The raw oils come in two kinds, vegetable and non-vegetable. Each month we decide how much of each raw oil to buy, how much to refine into the blend, how much to keep in storage, and which oils to include in that month's blend. Buying raw oils costs money at prices that vary by oil and by month, holding oil in storage incurs a per-ton monthly charge, and the finished product sells at a fixed price per ton. The objective is to maximize the total net profit over the whole horizon.

sets.name: m
sets.doc: the planning periods given as months in chronological order; the first member is the opening month and each later member follows the one before it, and the sequence is treated as a cycle so the month before the first month is the last month

sets.name: p
sets.doc: the raw oils available for refining and blending

sets.name: pv
sets.doc: the subset of raw oils that are vegetable oils

sets.name: pnv
sets.doc: the subset of raw oils that are non-vegetable oils; the member o3 is the non-vegetable oil that vegetable oils depend on

params.name: maxstore
params.doc: the maximum amount of any single raw oil that can be held in storage at the end of a month, in tons

params.name: maxusepv
params.doc: the maximum total amount of vegetable oils that can be refined in a single month, in tons

params.name: maxusepnv
params.doc: the maximum total amount of non-vegetable oils that can be refined in a single month, in tons

params.name: minusep
params.doc: the minimum amount of a raw oil that must be used in a month if that oil is used at all in that month, in tons

params.name: maxnusep
params.doc: the maximum number of distinct raw oils that may be used together in a single month's blend

params.name: sp
params.doc: the sales price of the final refined and blended oil, in dollars per ton

params.name: sc
params.doc: the storage cost charged for each ton of raw oil held in storage for one month, in dollars per ton per month

params.name: hmin
params.doc: the minimum allowed hardness of the final refined product, in hardness units

params.name: hmax
params.doc: the maximum allowed hardness of the final refined product, in hardness units

params.name: stock
params.doc: the required storage level of each raw oil at the end of the final month, in tons; this also represents the starting stock held before the horizon

params.name: h
params.doc: the hardness of each raw oil, in hardness units; hardness blends linearly by the amount of each oil used

params.name: cost
params.doc: the purchase cost of each raw oil in each month, in dollars per ton

For each raw oil and each month, the amount used must be zero unless the oil is marked as used, and when used it cannot exceed the monthly refining limit for that kind of oil, which is the vegetable limit for vegetable oils and the non-vegetable limit for non-vegetable oils.

For each month, the number of vegetable oils marked as used cannot exceed that dependency oil's used indicator scaled by the total number of vegetable oils, so if the dependency oil is not used then no vegetable oil may be used.

query: We lean awfully heavily on oil o2 month after month, and that single-supplier dependence makes the buyers nervous. Couldn't we just hold o2 in the blend to no more than 150 tons in every month and diversify the recipe? What's the optimizer weighing that keeps it piling o2 into batch after batch?

model_narrative: We run a food manufacturing operation that refines and blends raw oils into a final product over a sequence of months. The raw oils come in two kinds, vegetable and non-vegetable. Each month we decide how much of each raw oil to buy, how much to refine into the blend, how much to keep in storage, and which oils to include in that month's blend. Buying raw oils costs money at prices that vary by oil and by month, holding oil in storage incurs a per-ton monthly charge, and the finished product sells at a fixed price per ton. The objective is to maximize the total net profit over the whole horizon.

query: Within the non-vegetable side of the blend, oil o3 dominates and crowds out o2 in the early months, which feels lopsided for a single feedstock. Shouldn't o3 stay under 30 percent of the combined o2-plus-o3 going into each month's mix so the non-vegetable share is spread more evenly? What's the model seeing that makes it pile onto o3?
